steps.title: Identify what the slope represents
steps.content: The question asks for the increase in total fat for every 1 gram increase in total protein — that is the slope (rise / run) of the line of best fit. Read two points where the line crosses grid intersections: at $x = 10$ grams of protein, the line is at $y = 15$ grams of fat; at $x = 50$ grams of protein, the line is at $y = 75$ grams of fat.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
Evaluate all choices against slope = 1.5:
  • A: 2.5 — A slope of 2.5 would require fat to jump 2.5 g for every 1 g of protein, which is far steeper than the line shown. Incorrect.
  • B: 2.0 — A slope of 2.0 still overstates the steepness; the rise is not twice the run in this graph. Incorrect.
  • C: 1.5 — This matches the computed rise-over-run of 60/40 = 1.5. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: 1.0 — A slope of 1.0 would mean equal rise and run, but the line rises 60 units over a run of only 40 units. Incorrect.
